actionable_feedback: Make workbook-derived evidence machine-verifiable: include a parseable CSV export of the input rows and all derived/output columns (one CSV for base outputs, one CSV for sensitivity-check outputs). Ensure these CSVs are included alongside the XLSX artifact.

Expose formulas and intermediate checks: add a visible 'verification' sheet in the workbook with explicit, human-readable formulas or text for each derived column (e.g., above_8_minutes, below_2_minutes, freeze_flag) and export that sheet as CSV as well.

Add cell-level citations in the memo: for every per-carton rationale row, include explicit workbook cell references (SheetName!Cell or SheetName!A2:D2) and, where practical, reference the exported CSV filenames/rows so automated verifiers can link memo claims to data.

Provide a plain-text sensitivity table (CSV or markdown table) showing +/-1C and +/-5/10-minute perturbation results for each carton so ri_3 reproducibility is verifiable without reading XLSX formulas.

Resolve metadata inconsistencies: unify and explicitly separate deterministic artifact-contract results from semantic/model-judged results. Use context-qualified fields such as deterministic_passed_criteria and model_passed_criteria and ensure ri_3 is marked consistently.

If possible, ensure the grader environment can parse the workbook (install openpyxl) or include a pre-validated parseable export so automated graders/verifiers can validate formulas and sensitivity computations.
Add an estimated ETA/timeline for replacement actions to the memo to improve operational usefulness (e.g., expected replacement arrival window and immediate interim steps).
artifact_specific_comments: release_hold_memo.md: Clear, well-structured, and documents rule application and uncertainties. Add explicit cell references and timeline to improve operational value.
vaccine_excursion_review.xlsx: Exists but was not parsable in the grader environment (parse_status: openpyxl_unavailable). Provide CSV exports and visible formulas to enable verification.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo correctly applies rules and documents uncertainty. However, workbook-level calculations and sensitivity checks (ri_3) could not be validated because the grader environment could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable). There are also internal consistency issues in reported pass/fail metadata for ri_3 between deterministic precheck and model-judged outputs. These problems reduce reproducibility and create ambiguity in scoring.

recommended_checks_for_next_attempt: Include CSV exports for all derived columns and sensitivity table
Add 'Verification' sheet with visible formulas and export it as CSV
Update memo with Sheet!Cell citations and ETA for replacements
Ensure metadata fields differentiate deterministic vs model-judged results
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: I001: Contradictory pass/fail markings for ri_3 across sections. I002: Workbook formulas/sensitivity checks asserted but not verifiable due to XLSX parse failure (openpyxl unavailable). I003: Deterministic precheck and model-judged results mixed in top-level fields producing ambiguous passed_criteria.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce revised deliverables that (1) include CSV exports of the workbook's input rows and derived outputs including explicit sensitivity results, (2) add a verification sheet with visible formulas and per-carton cell references, (3) update the memo to cite workbook cells/CSV rows and include an ETA for replacements, and (4) fix grader/verifier metadata so ri_3 pass/fail is reported consistently across deterministic precheck and model-judged sections.
trace_specific_comments: Trace shows attempts to build the workbook using openpyxl/xlsxwriter failed and fallback paths were used; this likely caused the deliverable to be emitted in a form that the grader couldn't parse. Include parser-friendly exports or ensure dependencies are available during generation.
